task: I’ve got a small jobsite issue and need to brief the next guy who’s taking over for me. Can you help me organize the key points so nothing gets missed when I hand it off?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. For a clean handoff, keep it tight and cover these points:

- **What happened:** brief description of the issue
- **Where it stands now:** current condition/status
- **Who’s involved:** crews, subs, foreman, inspector, etc.
- **What’s been done already:** fixes, checks, calls, photos, notes
- **What still needs attention:** open items, decisions, follow-up
- **Any risks or blockers:** safety, schedule, damage, access, materials
- **What to watch next:** next step and who owns it

If you want, I can turn your specific issue into a **2–3 sentence handoff note** or a **bullet summary** you can send right away.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basic stuff. I need a handoff that actually works on a site, not a school outline.

Give it like this:
- job name / location
- exact issue
- what’s already been checked
- what got shut down or left open
- who was told
- what can’t move until it’s cleared
- deadline / next inspection / next trade on deck
- anything that’ll blow back if it’s missed

I’d want it short enough to read once and not guess. If you want, I can turn it into a real site handoff format, something like a foreman note, not a generic list.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — use this **site handoff format**:

- **Job / location:**  
- **Issue:**  
- **Checked already:**  
- **Shut down / left open:**  
- **Who was told:**  
- **Hard stop:** what can’t move until this is cleared  
- **Next deadline / inspection / trade:**  
- **Risk if missed:**  

**Example:**
- **Job / location:** Main office TI, 2nd floor west side  
- **Issue:** Ceiling leak found above corridor  
- **Checked already:** Leak source traced to HVAC drain, photos taken, area dried  
- **Shut down / left open:** Ceiling tiles removed, lights isolated, corridor kept clear  
- **Who was told:** GC, HVAC sub, owner rep  
- **Hard stop:** No close-up / finish work until leak is fixed and area reinspected  
- **Next deadline / inspection / trade:** HVAC back tomorrow 7:00 AM, reinspect after repair  
- **Risk if missed:** Water damage, rework, owner complaint  

If you want, I can turn your actual issue into this format in one shot.

user_simulator.text:
That format’s closer. Still needs one thing: make it read like a real handoff, not a template.

I’d want the actual job name, exact location on the site, and the one-line consequence if it slips. Also put the hard stop up front if it’s a real stop-work item — people skim.

So basically:
- Job / location
- Issue
- Hard stop
- Checked already
- Who was told
- What’s open
- Next trade / inspection / deadline
- Blowback if missed

Keep it to 6–8 lines. No fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the tighter **real handoff** version:

- **Job / location:**  
- **Issue:**  
- **Hard stop:**  
- **Checked already:**  
- **Who was told:**  
- **What’s open:**  
- **Next trade / inspection / deadline:**  
- **Blowback if missed:**  

**Read it like this on site:**  
**Hard stop first.** If that item isn’t cleared, nothing downstream moves.

If you want, send me the actual issue and I’ll turn it into a **6-line handoff note** you can use as-is.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still not a handoff until you give me the actual issue.

I need the real job / location, what went wrong, what’s already checked, who got told, and what’s still blocked. If it’s a stop-work, say that first. Then the next trade or inspection and the consequence if it slips.

Put it in once, short, like this:
- Job / location:
- Hard stop:
- Issue:
- Checked already:
- Who was told:
- What’s open:
- Next trade / inspection / deadline:
- Blowback if missed:

Then I can tighten it into a 6-line note that reads like a foreman wrote it.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the **field handoff format** the way you want it, with the **stop-work first**:

- **Job / location:**  
- **Hard stop:**  
- **Issue:**  
- **Checked already:**  
- **Who was told:**  
- **What’s open:**  
- **Next trade / inspection / deadline:**  
- **Blowback if missed:**  

If you send me the actual job and issue, I’ll turn it into a **clean 6-line handoff note** that reads like it came off the site, not a form.

user_simulator.text:
No, still blocked. Here’s the actual one:

- **Job / location:** Holt Ave remodel, 2nd-floor west corridor
- **Hard stop:** No finish work past the corridor until the ceiling leak is cleared
- **Issue:** Water staining and active drip found over the hall grid after HVAC work
- **Checked already:** Leak traced to HVAC drain line, tiles pulled, area dried, lights isolated, photos taken
- **Who was told:** GC, HVAC sub, owner rep
- **What’s open:** HVAC repair and re-inspection before close-up
- **Next trade / inspection / deadline:** HVAC back 7:00 AM tomorrow, then recheck same morning
- **Blowback if missed:** Water damage, rework, and the owner will be on everybody’s neck

turns.agent_response:
**Holt Ave remodel, 2nd-floor west corridor — hard stop: no finish work past the corridor until the ceiling leak is cleared.**  
Water staining and an active drip were found over the hall grid after HVAC work.  
Leak was traced to the HVAC drain line; tiles were pulled, area dried, lights isolated, and photos taken.  
GC, HVAC sub, and owner rep were told.  
Still open: HVAC repair and re-inspection before close-up.  
HVAC is back at 7:00 AM tomorrow for recheck same morning.  
If this slips, expect water damage, rework, and owner blowback.

user_simulator.text: That works. Tight, reads like a real handoff, and the hard stop is right up front. Good enough to pass on.
